title: Kenyan Fish Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful fish stew originating from Kenya. This recipe combines the rich flavors of Africa with the freshness of fish.

ingredients.name: Fish fillets (any white fish)
ingredients.notes: You can use any white fish such as tilapia, cod, or snapper.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Chopped onion adds a sweet and savory flavor to the stew.

ingredients.name: Tomatoes
ingredients.notes: Use fresh tomatoes for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Intensely spicy peppers
ingredients.notes: You can adjust the level of spiciness to your liking.

ingredients.name: Crunchy vegetable oil
ingredients.notes: Use a neutral-tasting oil with a high smoke point.

ingredients.name: Kenyan spices (e.g., cumin, coriander, turmeric)
ingredients.notes: You can adjust the amount to your liking.

ingredients.name: Salt and pepper
ingredients.notes: Add salt and pepper to taste.

ingredients.name: Fresh cilantro leaves
ingredients.notes: Use fresh cilantro leaves for garnish.

instructions: Heat the oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the tomatoes and cook until they are soft.
Add the fish fillets and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side.
Add the peppers, spices, salt, and pepper.
Stir well and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh cilantro leaves.
